column1: ## Phase C — JMH MERGE_OPERAND_SWEPT, native filter ON, two backstop variants
## ---- Phase C-1: filter ON, backstop ON (vt.merge.max.chain.length=64 default) ----

column10: 30s warmup - hits 100K but does not sustain

column1: ## C-1 vs Phase D baseline (for reference)
## ---- Phase C-2: filter ON, backstop OFF (-Dvenice.server.vt.merge.max.chain.length=0) ----
## C-2 gate evaluation (the "is the backstop redundant?" question)
## Without the backstop, throughput is dramatically higher (~140K vs ~74K) BUT operand chain p99 grows
## monotonically (39 -> 299 -> 551 -> 807) over the 3-iter run. The native compaction filter alone
## cannot keep chains bounded because compaction runs on RocksDB's natural schedule, not on demand.
## The synchronous backstop is the only mechanism that bounds chain depth deterministically.
## Recommendation: keep ChainLengthBackstop. Do NOT delete it as Phase C-2 was meant to evaluate.
